Hide Design Online button for stamps — StampEditor is the only design entry

category). Instead of fixing the mapping, hide the button entirely for
stamp slugs since the built-in StampEditor (Step 4) is the correct and
only design experience for stamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix auth middleware not applying to route plugins (Fastify encapsulation bug)

The auth preHandler hook was registered inside a scoped plugin, so it only
applied within that plugin's context — NOT to sibling route plugins (deals,
interview, report). This meant hubspotClient was always undefined, and
routes silently fell back to mock data instead of real HubSpot data.

Fix: wrap auth plugin with fastify-plugin (fp) to break encapsulation,
ensuring the auth hook applies globally to all registered routes.

Production-grade AI-powered AEC industry integration app using
Anthropic API with MCP (Model Context Protocol). Features:

- 4 roles: General Contractor, Architect/Engineer, Owner/Developer, Preconstruction
- Per-role MCP servers: Procore, Autodesk ACC, Primavera P6, Bluebeam, Oracle Aconex, CMiC ERP, STACK CT, BuildingConnected, Sage Estimating

- Recharts budget visualizations, sortable data tables, Export CSV
- ALERT / OVER BUDGET / NO COVERAGE / OVERDUE badge pills
- MULTI-SOURCE badge for cross-platform queries
- Tool call visualizer panel
- Command palette (⌘K), role keyboard shortcuts (⌘1-4)
- Config drawer with per-platform MCP URL/token settings

commit.message:
fix(data): apply strike_price.gte/lte at API level to get real greeks

Root cause: the chain snapshot endpoint returns greeks={} for deep ITM/OTM
contracts. Without a strike filter in the URL, the alphabetical sort gave
us calls starting at $500 (spot ~$672) — all deep ITM, all without greeks.
The result was 100% Black-Scholes fallback instead of native API greeks.

Fix: pass strike_price.gte and strike_price.lte (±30% of spot) directly in
the Massive API request URL, computed from the spot price already fetched
from the prev-close endpoint before the contracts loop begins.

- Contracts returned with greeks populated: 0/N → 693/693 (100%)
- BS fallback is now an emergency path for edge cases, not the main path
- fetch_spy_flip_universe() inherits the API-level ±30% filter; the
  redundant client-side _filter_strikes_around_spot call is removed
- Added "Gamma source: N API, M BS fallback" log line per fetch
- Deleted temporary diagnostic scripts: one_shot_inspect.py,
  test_greeks_endpoint.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

demo: fix pdfjs worker using local Vite ?url import instead of CDN

CDN URL (cdnjs.cloudflare.com) was unreachable at runtime, causing
"Setting up fake worker failed" error. Switch to a static Vite ?url
import so pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs is served locally from
node_modules in dev mode and bundled into dist/ for production.

Also add demo/src/vite-env.d.ts with `/// <reference types="vite/client" />`
so TypeScript understands the `?url` import suffix.

Fix pyproject.toml for twine compatibility

Use license = {text = ...} instead of PEP 639 SPDX string,
and cap hatchling <1.27.0 to avoid license-expression metadata
that current twine doesn't support. Remove license-files field.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(signing): use signMessage for job accept/deliver, fix 4 VDXF key addresses

Job accept and deliver were using signChallenge (CIdentitySignature format)
but the platform only verifies legacy signMessage format via verifymessage
RPC and bitcoinjs-message fallback. This caused silent signature failures.

Also fixes 4 agent VDXF key i-addresses (tags, website, avatar, category)
that didn't match getvdxfid output — data written under wrong addresses
would never be found by other applications.

Relax overoptimized filters that were killing trade frequency

- ADX filter: 20 -> 25 (M5 noise makes 20 too restrictive)
- RSI thresholds: 30/70 -> 35/65 (redundant with Z-Score 2.5)
- ATR max multiple: 1.7 -> 2.2 (was blocking the volatile
  conditions that create Z-Score extremes)
- Tight trail: 0.8 -> 1.0 ATR (0.8 clipped by M5 noise)
- Hard TP: 3.0 -> 5.0 ATR (safety net, not a target — let runners run)
- Daily loss limit: 4% -> 6% (one 10%-risk trade could hit 4%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Human-AI Task Command System to goals page

Complete memory-aid + AI-writable task management system:

UI (goals.html):
- Memory strip: last-visit gap tracker with color alert (green/amber/red by days elapsed)
- Daily Focus panel: top 3 priority tasks always visible at page top
- Brain Dump Zone: instant idea capture (Ctrl+Enter to save), queue with promote-to-task
- AI Task Command Center: paste Claude JSON/text output → auto-parse → task added
- TODAY / SHORT-TERM / LONG-TERM sections with priority sorting and task counts
- Completed archive (collapsed by default)
- Memory Aids section: sticky note guide, voice command reference, dashboard URL for Lindy
- Each task card: expand/collapse, per-step checkboxes with persistent state (localStorage)
- Step drill-down: click step to reveal full detail + pitfalls (⚠ Common Mistakes)
- Progress bar per task (X/N steps complete)
- MOVE / COMPLETE / DELETE actions; local state survives page refresh

Backend (scripts/task_manager.py):
- CLI: python task_manager.py add "title" --category today --priority critical
- Python API: add_task(), complete_task(), list_tasks(), add_brain_dump()
- Auto-generates step templates from title keywords (api/setup/trading/security)
- process_ai_payload() for GitHub Actions dispatch integration

Workflow (.github/workflows/add-task.yml):
- Triggered by repository_dispatch event_type='add_task'
- Lindy AI, voice assistants, local A9 AI all POST to this endpoint
- Task appears on dashboard within ~60 seconds of voice command

Data (data/tasks.json):
- Pre-seeded with 7 real actionable tasks (critical security + high priority setup)
- Full step-by-step guides with pitfalls for each task

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Labor: clock-in time and hours same size, same line

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve iOS Bluetooth adapter state detection for Field Link sessions

On iOS, CoreBluetooth's CBCentralManager initially emits `unknown` while
initializing (~100-500ms). Using `.first` on the adapter state stream
captured this stale state and incorrectly treated Bluetooth as unavailable.
Fix filters out `unknown` states before evaluating, with timeout fallback.

Also updates GitHub org references (RedGridTactical → RedGridMGRS) and
refreshes store listing release notes for v1.2.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
docs: add descriptions to screenshots section in README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Prevent failed emails from being permanently lost behind Graph delta cursor

- isMessageProcessed() now excludes result='error' rows from dedup check,
  allowing failed messages to be retried on the next poll cycle
- fetchEmailsViaGraph() no longer advances the delta cursor when any message
  in the cycle failed processing, ensuring the next poll replays the same
  delta batch so error messages get another chance

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move email to Footer Connect section, remove from About

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

audit R19: fix edge supersession validation, authority lock holding, bus close timeout

- store/memory: validate EdgeSuperseded content type before mutating indices
  (matches edge.created fail-fast pattern); return EdgeIndexError on bad content
- authority: release RLock before calling trustModel.Score in Evaluate,
  preventing deadlock risk and matching DelegationChain pattern
- bus: add 30s timeout to Close() to prevent indefinite hang on blocking handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
